name: Housing Foundation Of America, Greensboro Branch
description_short: HUD-certified nonprofit providing free homebuyer education, credit counseling, and down payment assistance across multiple southeastern states.
description_long:
Housing Foundation of America, Inc. is a HUD-approved 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization that has operated for over 10 years, serving as a leading provider of housing counseling and homebuyer assistance. The organization operates across multiple states including Florida, North Carolina, and serves geographic areas including Miami-Dade, Broward, Palm Beach, Guilford, Mecklenburg, Forsyth, Wake, Orange, and Lee counties.

HFoA offers comprehensive free housing counseling services including pre-purchase and post-purchase homebuyer counseling, credit and budget education, foreclosure prevention assistance, home repair guidance, and down payment assistance programs. They provide both in-person (now virtual) workshops, an 8-hour Fannie Mae Framework certificate course, landlord certification training, and online homebuyer club meetings. Their services address mortgage delinquency resolution, debt management, loss mitigation, and help consumers navigate property listings and down payment options.

The organization distinguishes itself through HUD certification, partnerships with lenders and real estate professionals, property listing resources with census tract geomaps, and documented success stories such as clients receiving substantial down payment grants ($74,000+ documented). They offer Fannie Mae-certified training and maintain a robust toolkit including homebuyer essentials, home inspection resources, and foreclosure scam prevention education.

However, the website indicates the main site "is not activated" and provides limited specific contact information beyond a phone number (954-923-5001). Geographic service areas appear concentrated in southeastern states, and the website lacks detailed information about eligibility requirements, wait times, or specific assistance amounts available by location.
pros: HUD-certified nonprofit with 10+ years operating history and 501(c)(3) status
Free comprehensive homebuyer counseling covering pre-purchase, post-purchase, and loss mitigation
Documented down payment assistance programs with real client success stories ($74,000+ grants awarded)
Fannie Mae Framework certified 8-hour training courses for first-time homebuyers
Multi-state operations serving 10+ counties across Florida and North Carolina
Credit and budget counseling courses including establishing/rebuilding credit and saving basics
Foreclosure prevention services and scam avoidance education
cons: Website states 'This website is not activated,' suggesting limited online functionality and information access
Geographic service limited to southeastern states (Florida and North Carolina primarily)
Limited publicly available contact information or specific eligibility criteria on website
No details on wait times, application process, or expected assistance amounts by program
Website navigation appears duplicated/poorly structured with minimal detail in service descriptions
best_for: First-time homebuyers seeking free HUD-certified counseling and down payment assistance in southeastern states
Homeowners facing foreclosure or mortgage delinquency needing loss mitigation and prevention counseling
Consumers rebuilding credit who need structured budget and credit education courses
Landlords seeking certification training in covered service areas
diagnosis: Housing Foundation of America is ideal for first-time homebuyers and homeowners in Florida and North Carolina seeking free HUD-certified counseling, credit education, and down payment assistance. The main caveat is that the website infrastructure appears incomplete ('not activated'), limiting online accessibility, so direct phone contact (954-923-5001) is necessary to determine eligibility and service availability in your specific location.
services: Credit essentials and establishing/rebuilding credit courses
Basic savings education and budgeting workshops
Down payment assistance programs and alerts
Foreclosure prevention and loss mitigation counseling
Mortgage delinquency and default resolution counseling
Home repair and home inspection guidance tools
Property listing resources with census tract geomaps
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 122 N Elm St Ste 525, Greensboro, NC 27401
